spec.title: CPU Busy %
spec.description: convert to CPU % (inverted idle rate). Your primary bottleneck signal.

spec.title: pg_stat_bgwriter_checkpoints_req_total
spec.description:
forced Postgres checkpoints signal the DB is being overwhelmed by writes faster than it can flush.
  Given the write-heavy trigger workload, this is a direct health indicator.

spec.title: go_memstats_heap_inuse_bytes
spec.description:
This tells you whether it's the Go
  process consuming that memory or something else.

spec.title: node_network_receive_bytes_total
spec.description: as a crawler, inbound network traffic directly tracks crawl activity and explains load spikes in context.

spec.title: node_load1
spec.description: node_load1

spec.title: DB Pool Usage Ratio
spec.description: bee_db_pool_usage_ratio

spec.title: Crawler P95
spec.description: Overall p95 crawler duration from histogram buckets.

spec.title: Crawler Phase Rate
spec.description: Crawler phase executions per second split by phase and outcome.

spec.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroup(completed_at, $__interval) as time,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'completed') as "Completed",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failed') as "Failed",
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'skipped') as "Skipped"
FROM tasks
WHERE $__timeFilter(completed_at)
  AND completed_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY time
ORDER BY time;

SELECT
  $__timeGroup(completed_at, $__interval) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT job_id) AS "Jobs"
FROM tasks
WHERE $__timeFilter(completed_at)
  AND completed_at IS NOT NULL
  AND status = 'completed'
GROUP BY time
ORDER BY time;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT (interval '1 millisecond') * $__interval_ms AS step
),
buckets AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    date_bin((SELECT step FROM params), $__timeFrom()::timestamptz, TIMESTAMPTZ '1970-01-01'),
    $__timeTo()::timestamptz,
    (SELECT step FROM params)
  ) AS time
),
alive AS (
  SELECT b.time, j.id AS job_id
  FROM buckets b
  JOIN jobs j
    ON j.started_at <= b.time
   AND (j.completed_at IS NULL OR j.completed_at > b.time)
),
producing AS (
  SELECT
    date_bin((SELECT step FROM params), completed_at, TIMESTAMPTZ '1970-01-01') AS time,
    job_id
  FROM tasks
  WHERE $__timeFilter(completed_at)
    AND status IN ('completed','failed','skipped')
  GROUP BY 1, 2
)
SELECT
  a.time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.job_id) FILTER (WHERE p.job_id IS NULL) AS "Jobs idle (no task activity)",
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.job_id) AS "Jobs alive"
FROM alive a
LEFT JOIN producing p
  ON p.time = a.time AND p.job_id = a.job_id
GROUP BY a.time
ORDER BY a.time;

spec.title: DB SQL Latency Heatmap
spec.description: Raw DB SQL latency bucket distribution

spec.title: Dispatch rate by outcome
spec.description: see ok vs err vs capacity vs paced composition over time

spec.title: Dispatch success ratio
spec.description: single-glance health — are dispatches succeeding?

spec.title: Dispatch rate per job
spec.description: spot which job is dominating throughput

spec.title: Scheduled ZSET depth
spec.description: Tasks sitting in the scheduler. Climbs when dispatcher is lagging or when a huge sitemap just landed (expected temporarily). Concerning only if it climbs and stays high.

spec.title: Consumer pending
spec.description: Messages read from stream but not ack'd. Should return to ~0 between batches. Non-zero with autoclaim_total rising = workers dying mid-task.

spec.title: Outbox oldest age
spec.description: Age of the oldest outbox row that's due for dispatch (run_at <= NOW()). Healthy: 0. Climbing = sweeper isn't keeping up, Redis unreachable, or worker stalled.

spec.title: pg_stat_activity_xact_runtime
spec.description: active transaction duration in Postgres. Long values = lock contention.

spec.title: http_server_request_duration_seconds_bucket
spec.description: p95 request latency from user perspective.

spec.title: http_status_codes_total
spec.description: filter to 5xx rate. Catches errors that don't appear in task logs.

spec.title: go_goroutines
spec.description: active goroutines. Spikes or sustained growth indicates worker pile-up.

spec.title: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes
spec.description: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes
